task.title: Phase 2 Exercise: Maps, Keys, and Content Reuse
task.shortdesc: Build a DITA map to organize your TaskFlow topics, then practice conref and keyref reuse patterns. Required for both technical writers and software engineers.

ul.li: Completed Phase 2 Exercise: Writing Your First DITA Topics
Read Part 3 topic: Reuse, Keys, and Variant Management
Three TaskFlow topics from the previous exercise

context.p: You will assemble your TaskFlow topics into a navigable project, define reusable keys, and create a conref library — the fundamental patterns that make DITA powerful for content reuse at scale.

ul.li: Define a <keydef> for the product name "TaskFlow"
Organize topics under <topichead> groups: "Getting Started" and "Reference"

info.codeblock:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <!DOCTYPE map PUBLIC "-//OASIS//DTD DITA Map//EN" "map.dtd"> <map id="taskflow-map"> <title>TaskFlow Documentation</title>
<!-- Key definitions --> <keydef keys="product-name"> <topicmeta><keywords><keyword>TaskFlow</keyword></keywords></topicmeta> </keydef>
<!-- TODO: Add topichead groups and topicrefs -->
</map>

step.cmd: Replace hardcoded product names with <keyword keyref="product-name"/> in your topics.

info.p: Find every instance of the word "TaskFlow" in your three topic files and replace them with a key reference:
This means if the product is renamed, you only change the keydef in the map.
info.note: Writer focus: This is how enterprise teams manage product rebranding across thousands of topics — one change propagates everywhere. Engineer focus: Key resolution happens during DITA-OT preprocessing. The keyref module resolves these before the transform runs.

step.cmd: Build the complete map to HTML5.

stepresult.p: You should get a multi-page HTML5 site with navigation. Every "TaskFlow" reference should render correctly from the keydef. Open out/taskflow-site/index.html.

info.p: A conref warehouse is a topic that holds reusable content fragments. Create one with:

ul.li: A reusable <note> for system requirements (give it an id)

A reusable

for the support contact (give it an id)
A reusable <step> for "Verify installation" (give it an id)

info.codeblock:
Reusable Content Library Requires Java 17+ and 512 MB available disk space.

Contact support at support@taskflow.example.com.

step.cmd: Use conref to pull the system requirements note into your installation task.

info.p: Rebuild and verify the note appears in the output.

stepresult.p: The system requirements note should appear in the installation task, pulled from the warehouse. Edit the warehouse text and rebuild — the change should propagate automatically.

step.cmd: Create a new task topic taskflow-docs/upgrading-taskflow.dita that reuses content.

ul.li: Use conref for system requirements (same note as install topic)
Use <keyword keyref="product-name"/> for the product name
Add this new topic to your map

stepresult.p: Rebuild the map. The new topic should appear in navigation and show the same reusable content as the install topic.

step.cmd: Test that reuse is working: change the product name in the keydef and rebuild.

info.p: In your map, change the keydef keyword from "TaskFlow" to "TaskFlow Pro". Rebuild and verify that every topic now shows "TaskFlow Pro" without editing any topic files.

stepresult.p: All topics should display "TaskFlow Pro". Change it back to "TaskFlow" when done.

p.b: Self-Check — you should now be able to:

ul.li: ✓ Create a DITA map with topichead groups and topicrefs
✓ Define and use keydefs for product names
✓ Create a conref warehouse with reusable fragments
✓ Pull content via conref into multiple topics
✓ Build a multi-topic site from a map
✓ Understand how reuse enables single-source publishing
